title: Mantou (Chinese Steamed Buns)

ingredients.title: For the Dough:
items: 500g all-purpose flour (or 00 flour for a softer texture)

instruction.title: Activating the Yeast
steps: Mix warm water and sugar in a bowl until dissolved.
Sprinkle yeast over the surface. Let it sit 5–10 minutes until frothy.

instruction.title: Making the Dough
steps: In a large mixing bowl, combine flour and salt.
Gradually add the yeast mixture while stirring with a wooden spoon or dough hook.
Knead the dough for 10–12 minutes (or 6–8 min in a stand mixer) until smooth and elastic.
Incorporate oil, kneading until fully absorbed.

instruction.title: First Proof
steps: Form the dough into a ball, place in a lightly oiled bowl, and cover with a damp cloth.
Let it rise in a warm place for 1–1.5 hours, or until doubled in size.

instruction.title: Shaping the Mantou
steps: Punch down the dough and turn it onto a lightly floured surface.
Roll into a log (~30 cm) and cut into 12 equal pieces.
Shape each piece into a smooth bun: tuck edges underneath and roll gently.

instruction.title: Second Proof
steps: Place buns on parchment squares in a bamboo steamer or metal steamer tray.
Cover with a cloth and let rise 30–40 minutes until puffy.

instruction.title: Steaming
steps: Bring water in a steamer pot to a rolling boil.
Steam buns over medium-high heat for 12–15 minutes.
Turn off heat and let buns sit for 3–5 minutes before removing the lid (prevents collapse).

instruction.title: Serving
steps: Optionally brush with milk or water and sprinkle sesame seeds.
Serve warm, plain, or with sweet/savory fillings.

chefTips: For extra fluffy buns, replace 50g of water with milk.
Smooth buns create a cloud-like appearance; avoid cracks.
Do not overcrowd the steamer; buns expand during cooking.
Add condensed milk or pumpkin puree for flavor variations.
